steps.title: Compare the two explanations
steps.content: Text 1 links backlash mainly to public art that is not aesthetically and conceptually bland. Text 2 says reactions also depend on context, especially whether challenging public art is integrated into surroundings that prepare viewers to appreciate it.

steps.title: Reject overbroad responses
steps.content: Choice A denies the role of unconventionality too strongly. Choice B says only universally appealing art belongs in public spaces. Choice C focuses on civic leaders and conventional art, which Text 2 does not address. Context affects reception along with the work's attributes. The answer is D.
